methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
ADASI is a sovereign-funded frontier-tech company operating in the Defense & Security sector at the EDGE stage. The comparable universe spans 10 peers—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI—drawn from a broader Defense & Security cohort of 21 companies.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable in this case. While 21 same-sector peers exist, the narrow filters required for robust triangulation—matching stage, adjacency, and valuation tier—yielded fewer than three datapoints across all combinations. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce material noise given ADASI's specialized positioning and sovereign-funding structure, which differentiates it from commercial Defense & Security peers.

The absence of sufficient narrow-stage comparables reflects the scarcity of EDGE-stage companies in the Defense & Security frontier-tech space. Without meeting the three-datapoint threshold, traditional relative valuation cannot be applied with confidence. A complete valuation would require alternative approaches—such as revenue multiple analysis if ADASI's financials are available, or discounted cash flow modeling based on program-level visibility—and deeper diligence into how the sovereign-funding structure and defense applications affect pricing relative to commercial peers. The key uncertainty remains the appropriate valuation multiple for an early-stage, publicly-backed defense technology company.
